item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Solid article over all! Its a fun read and doesn't take itself too seriously. I dont love the usage of lesbian_gengar, behavior is very out of character for her. But otherwise its fun and the hook grabbed me fast. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am not be an experienced writer, so maybe my opinion is not the best. But I think that the foundation pretending to be a fiction website, just like it is for us irl, building a link between truth and fiction is a great idea, but that I initially thought it was too great to work. I'm saying that this is a premise that I expected to fail absolutely or even sound somewhat cringe, and I bet many people thought about something like this but came out too weird, but your article came out great. I loved the way it was written, the way that chat logs were built and how it looks so real. I really enjoyed, thanks for the author for this :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea teased that the only anomalous thing is the "SCP Foundation - Site 50" entry itself, everything else is just the Streisand effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Plus discord logs everything to their servers anyway, vore-tracker is redundant, just infiltrate discord. Even if it does potentially making a deal with Eris, if 5721 is to be believed. GAW isn't perfect but they're better at infosec than this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if I like GAW being on discord. It's too mainstream, I don't think they would want to make themselves so easy to find. That said, it's only a minor complaint. The rest of the article was good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The crux of Parawatch is that they have no power or agency. They are just zany conspiracy theorists who happen to have some of the right information about this insane world just behind the veil.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Parawatch doesn't know about the Foundation? I don't understand your point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
there's a bit of canonical weirdness here: what, Parawatch is A-OK to leave up and running, but this is an actual risk of a BK?

ehh, whatever, the jokey tone of this makes up for it. maybe the senior researcher on 5304 needs to have a talk with the suits in charge of monitoring Parawatch.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love SCPs that have social media interactions and conversations and it was great how real it felt. I especially loved the clickbait youtube guys. Also, there's just not enough GAW on this site.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was beautiful, and I especially loved the parts where interactions on social media platforms (especially Discord) were incorporated into the article; you did that very well. The interactions between GAW members were well-written and made me laugh quite a bit (even if they were a bit contradictory to established traits of certain members).

+1 c:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is pretty out of character for GAW to pull a stunt like this when that's like, the last thing they'd want to do. Pissing off the Foundation and presenting them as powerless to stop their shenanigans and present them as weak is a pretty poor portrayal for both of them.

Which is a shame because if the central narrative was altered just a bit to make it as a joke that GAW took too far and are having issues trying to deal with the repercussions of their actions, it would sell the article a lot more than its current form.

item.title: Upvoted here and on r/SCP
item.content:encoded: I love this kind of self-referential stuff. The TV Tropes -J is another great example of real life and fiction bleeding into each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm tempted to plus one solely for the effort gone through of mocking up the Discord and reddit caps, but it's hard to do much with GAW these days and this doesn't compel me in any particular way. Neutral on this one, at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hey, this is pretty sweet! i thought the discord-esque chat logs were a nice touch, and a fair bit easier to read. while i can understand that this is fairly out of line with GAW ""canon"" (e.g SCP-4493 as ARD mentioned), i'm personally not a huge fan of strict canons, so i found this to be a nice change of pace.
also "hello yes i am a normal internet user definitely not a fed" is based

+1

item.title: Re: alternately titled: What Happened to Site-50?
item.content:encoded: (author here on a new account! the openmaps link under imagecred should read: https://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright)

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Thank you :]
First SCP I've read in a while that actually made me laugh, truly excellent job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rounder and ARD summed up my feelings better than I could, particularly with regards to the whole 'memes as a substitute for humour' thing you have going on. Reluctant downvote, because although I like the premise and appreciate the amount of effort you put into it, the execution just wasn't carried through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even ignoring that the Foundation let their bot get caught so easily, poking them at all, much less this aggressively, is exactly the sort of thing that bluntfiend and lesbian_gengar would both want to avoid. Especially when you consider articles like SCP-4493, that explicitly indicate that Jude is keeping GAW from drawing public attention to avoid bringing the Foundation down on them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this carries a somewhat interesting premise and trips over its shoelaces before driving it into the ground. the idea of the foundation dealing with an extremely minor but very present threat to their infosec is cool — but the article kind of shoots its own comedic potential in the foot.

the screenshots take more from the article than they add; they disrupt the flow of storytelling for the discord logs, and if they're trying to be funny they're too self-referency for that. they're not jokes, because they don't have setups and punchlines; they're obtuse references to internet zeitgeist. it's like a dude standing next to you the whole time poking you in the ribs every time and saying "haha look thats supposed to riff on [X]". the gaw logs are… acceptable, if nondescript.

i generally don't mind stories where the foundation is amazingly incompetent but i take issue with this because it almost feels… campy? this group of internet teenagers manage to be sharper and smarter than this obviously gigantic paramilitary group capable of scraping some of the biggest sites on the internet. it feels like they're amazingly stupid — the reddit username, having their bot be so easily traceable — so that GAW gets the last laugh in the story, so that they 'win'. i have no problem with a goi getting one over on the foundation in a void — but this pulls back the curtain way too far to enjoy. when you make one character really smart and the other really dumb, it's harder than you'd think to make the smart one's victory actually feel victorious.

I honestly never thought I’d see the day this is used in an SCP but here it is. This is an incredibly nice and clean read to me, excellent work as always!

#JusticeForVorebot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hilarious. I love the idea that the Foundation set up a bot called Vorebot, and the social media snippets are very well done and really make this article. +1 :D
You madlad, I applaud you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gave you my commends a few times over in IRC, this was a great read and I thoroughly enjoyed all of it. I'm glad to have critted this and I'm happy to see it up here.

Great job, +1

item.title: alternately titled: What Happened to Site-50?
item.content:encoded:
Barbra Streisand
(Woo woo woo woo woo woo woo)
(Woo woo woo woo woo woo woo)
(Woo woo woo woo woo woo woo)
Barbra Streisand

Thanks to Tanhony, Cyvstvi13, Iszth, PlaguePJP, Tyumen, redredred, barredowl, Deadly Bread, and JaonHax for crit.

HUGE thanks to Calibri Bold and TheeSherm for letting me turn them into clickbait thumbnails. Special thanks to daviiid for being my fwiend. :]

click the link site-50 is a real thing. shoutout to the random newbie in #site17 who informed me of this. I will make a serious authorpost on the day I die

Image cred:

+ show block
– hide block

the use of a google maps image has been properly attributed as per this guide: https://www.google.com/permissions/geoguidelines/attr-guide/
gaycopmp4's icon: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Night_Fox.svg
trainerDP's icon: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:How_to_report_someone_on_discord.png
lesbian_gengar's icon: http://www.scp-wiki.net/rainbow-crapitalism
solidsnakeasscheekannihilator's icon: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Metal_Gear_Rising_promotion_art.jpg
r/dankmemes image source, edited by me: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Twemoji12_1f633.svg

Youtube thumbnails are edited pictures of Calibri Bold and TheeSherm, released with permission.
Background of thumbnail 1 is a screencap of https://www.openstreetmap.org.
Background of thumbnail 2 is this image: https://ccsearch.creativecommons.org/photos/78376c0b-9c60-47ab-ab50-8d2e1c22d259
Emoji sources: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Twemoji12_1f631.svg, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Twemoji12_1f47d.svg, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Twemoji12_1f640.svg, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Twemoji12_1f64a.svg

pretty much everything else was made by me with inspect element :D
